title: Effect of changes in policies and economic conditions on the foreign exchange market
description: Practice your understanding of how exchange rates change when economic conditions change.
topic_path: Open economy: international trade and finance
Effect of changes in policies and economic conditions on the foreign exchange market
Effect of changes in policies and economic conditions on the foreign exchange market

assessment_items.question_content:
One of Ghana’s top trading partners is India. The currency in Ghana is the cedi and the currency in India is the rupee. 

**Which of the following is a likely consequence of an increase in business optimism in Ghana?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a depreciation of the rupee against the cedi
lower real interest rates in Ghana
a depreciation of the cedi against the rupee
an increase in the demand for loanable funds in India
an increase in the supply of loanable funds in Ghana

assessment_items.question_content:
As a result of an economic boom in Hamsterville, foreign investors want to buy more financial assets from Hamsterville.

**Which of the following graphs correctly illustrates the impact this will have on the foreign exchange market for the Hamsterville Snark (SN)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled dollar per S N, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q S N. One upward-sloping line from the bottom left to the top right is labeled S subscript 1. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled D subscript 1 and D subscript 2, with D subscript 2 above D subscript 1. Two points mark the intersections of S subscript 1 with D subscript 1 and D subscript 2. A horizontal dashed line extends rightward from E R subscript 1 on the vertical axis to the lower intersection. Another horizontal dashed line rightward from E R subscript 2 on the vertical axis to the higher intersection. A right-pointing arrow is placed between the two points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0425e9e46f5ab4e570c0abfbd3ce861ca0994e3)

![A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled S N per dollar, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q dollar. One downward-sloping line is labeled D subscript dollar. Two upward-sloping lines from the bottom left to the top right are labeled S subscript dollar subscript 1 and S subscript dollar subscript 2, with S subscript dollar subscript 2 below S subscript dollar subscript 1. Two points mark the intersections of D subscript dollar with S subscript dollar subscript 1 and S subscript dollar subscript 2. A horizontal dashed line extends rightward from E R subscript 1 on the vertical axis to the upper intersection. Another horizontal dashed line extends rightward from E R subscript 2 on the vertical axis to the lower intersection. A right-pointing arrow is placed between the two points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f4b2df73b5f1baedb36d1bbe7445160fc9063556)

![A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled dollar per S N, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript S N. One upward-sloping line from the bottom left to the top right is labeled S subscript 1. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled D subscript 1 and D subscript 2, with D subscript 1 above D subscript 2. Two points mark the intersections of S subscript 1 with D subscript 1 and D subscript 2. A horizontal dashed line extends rightward from E R subscript 1 on the vertical axis to the higher intersection. Another horizontal dashed line rightward from E R subscript 2 on the vertical axis to the lower intersection. A left-pointing arrow is placed between the two points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3ad7168d18b889da19243036b2f70e92a5fb661)

![A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled S N per dollar, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q S N. One downward-sloping line is labeled D subscript S N. Two upward-sloping lines from the bottom left to the top right are labeled S subscript S N subscript 1 and S subscript S N subscript 2, with S subscript S N subscript 2 below S subscript S N subscript 1. Two points mark the intersections of D subscript S N with S subscript S N subscript 1 and S subscript S N subscript 2. A horizontal dashed line extends rightward from E R subscript 1 on the vertical axis to the upper intersection. Another horizontal dashed line extends rightward from E R subscript 2 on the vertical axis to the lower intersection. A right-pointing arrow is placed between the two points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5676d45a2c82e0778af297431d71098f6315bf66)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: D subscript S N, S subscript S N 1, and S subscript S N 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript S N, and the vertical axis is labeled dollar sign / S N. The axes are unmarked. Line D subscript S N is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S subscript S N 1 and S subscript S N 2 are upward-sloping, with S subscript S N 2 to the right of S subscript S N 1. Line S subscript S N 1 intersects D subscript S N at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Line S subscript S N 2 intersects D subscript S N at a lower point, labeled E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A rightward arrow points from S subscript S N 1 to S subscript S N 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ba866db336b0c1cf68dd8498684f7100819d40ce)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is most likely to cause a currency to depreciate while discouraging capital formation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the demand for loanable funds.
An increase in the supply of loanable funds
An increase in the supply of money
A decrease in the demand for money
A budget surplus

assessment_items.question_content:
The currency of Japan is the yen, and the currency of the United States is the U.S. dollar.


**Which of the following graphs depicts the impact on the exchange rate for the yen if the United States imposes a tariff on goods from Japan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: S subscript yen, D subscript yen 1, and D subscript yen 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen, and the vertical axis is labeled dollar sign / yen. The axes are unmarked. Line S subscript yen is upward-sloping. Lines D subscript yen 1 and D subscript yen 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with D subscript yen 2 to the left of D subscript yen 1. Line D subscript yen 1 intersects S subscript yen at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Line D subscript yen 2 intersects S subscript yen at a lower point, labeled E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A leftward arrow points from D subscript yen 1 to D subscript yen 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6ce61d502729db693213802076a8eb4ab3755043)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: D subscript yen 2, S subscript yen, and S subscript yen 1. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen, and the vertical axis is labeled dollar sign / yen. The axes are unmarked. Line D subscript yen 2 is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S subscript yen and S subscript yen 1 are upward-sloping, with S subscript yen 1 to the left of S subscript yen. Line S subscript yen 1 intersects D subscript yen 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Line S subscript yen intersects D subscript yen 2 at a lower point, labeled E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A rightward arrow points from S subscript yen 1 to S subscript yen.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/faf667f454163e2d9ae275bd11e7f732ec73f4a8)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: D subscript dollar sign, S subscript dollar sign 1, and S subscript dollar sign 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript dollar sign, and the vertical axis is labeled yen / dollar sign. The axes are unmarked. Line D subscript dollar sign is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S subscript dollar sign 1 and S subscript dollar sign 2 are upward-sloping, with S subscript dollar sign 2 to the left of S subscript dollar sign 1. Line S subscript dollar sign 1 intersects D subscript dollar sign at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Line S subscript dollar sign 2 intersects D subscript dollar sign at a higher point, labeled E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A leftward arrow points from S subscript dollar sign 1 to S subscript dollar sign 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/515931c4626d728b06f4426dc1ab413267a755c8)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: D subscript yen, S subscript yen 1, and S subscript yen 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen, and the vertical axis is labeled yen / dollar sign. The axes are unmarked. Line D subscript yen is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S subscript yen 1 and S subscript yen 2 are upward-sloping, with S subscript yen 2 to the left of S subscript yen 1. Line S subscript yen 1 intersects D subscript yen at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Line S subscript yen 2 intersects D subscript yen at a higher point, labeled E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A leftward arrow points from S subscript yen 1 to S subscript yen 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb3415e0d23c94fd0bad460eab15e742ba9e9f45)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: S subscript yen, D subscript yen 1, and D subscript yen 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen, and the vertical axis is labeled dollar sign / yen. The axes are unmarked. Line S subscript yen is upward-sloping. Lines D subscript yen 1 and D subscript yen 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with D subscript yen 2 to the right of D subscript yen 1. Line D subscript yen 1 intersects S subscript yen at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Line D subscript yen 2 intersects S subscript yen at a lower point, labeled E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A rightward arrow points from D subscript yen 1 to D subscript yen 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cebe94caded6d99d8d1824f856a32e8f0b6eac71)

assessment_items.question_content:
The currency of Japan is the yen, and the currency of the United States is the U.S. dollar.

**Which of the following graphs depicts the impact on the exchange rate for the U.S. dollar if the United States imposes a tariff on goods from Japan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript s, and the vertical axis is labeled yen per dollar. The axes are unmarked. One downward-sloping line from top left to bottom right is labeled D subscript s. Two upward-sloping lines from bottom left to top right are labeled S subscript s 1 and S subscript s 2, with line S subscript s 2 to the left of S subscript s 1. Line D subscript s intersects line S subscript s 1 at a black point labeled E R subscript 1, and intersects line S subscript s 2 at a black point labeled E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A green left-pointing arrow between S subscript s 1 and S subscript s 2 indicates a shift from S subscript s 1 to S subscript s 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/11ffeca005b16d0375dc0c49e3f8ce53d642ecfb)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points of intersection. The vertical axis is labeled dollar sign per yen, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen. One upward-sloping line is labeled S subscript yen. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled D subscript yen 1 and D subscript yen 2, with D subscript yen 2 to the left of D subscript yen 1. Lines S subscript yen and D subscript yen 1 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 1. Lines S subscript yen and D subscript yen 2 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 2. Dashed lines extend horizontally from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the intersections. A left arrow points from D subscript yen 1 to D subscript yen 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6ce61d502729db693213802076a8eb4ab3755043)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points of intersection. The vertical axis is labeled dollar sign per yen, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen. One downward-sloping line is labeled D subscript yen 2. Two upward-sloping lines are labeled S subscript yen and S subscript yen 1, with S subscript yen 1 to the left of S subscript yen. Lines D subscript yen 2 and S subscript yen intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 2. Lines D subscript yen 2 and S subscript yen 1 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 1. Dashed lines extend horizontally from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the intersections. A right arrow points from S subscript yen to S subscript yen 1.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/faf667f454163e2d9ae275bd11e7f732ec73f4a8)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points of intersection. The vertical axis is labeled yen per dollar sign, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen. One downward-sloping line is labeled D subscript yen. Two upward-sloping lines are labeled S subscript yen 1 and S subscript yen 2, with S subscript yen 2 to the left of S subscript yen 1. Lines D subscript yen and S subscript yen 1 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 1. Lines D subscript yen and S subscript yen 2 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 2. Dashed lines extend horizontally from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the intersections. A left arrow points from S subscript yen 1 to S subscript yen 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb3415e0d23c94fd0bad460eab15e742ba9e9f45)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points of intersection. The vertical axis is labeled dollar sign per yen, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript yen. One upward-sloping line is labeled S subscript yen. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled D subscript yen 1 and D subscript yen 2, with D subscript yen 2 to the right of D subscript yen 1. Lines S subscript yen and D subscript yen 1 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 1. Lines S subscript yen and D subscript yen 2 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 2. Dashed lines extend horizontally from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the intersections. A right arrow points from D subscript yen 1 to D subscript yen 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cebe94caded6d99d8d1824f856a32e8f0b6eac71)

assessment_items.question_content:
The euro is the currency of the European Union (E.U.).

**If the (E.U.) imposes a quota limiting imports from the United States, what will happen to the market for Euros and the exchange rate for the Euro?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Supply of the euro decreases; the euro appreciates
Supply of the euro increases; the euro appreciates
Demand for euro increases; euro appreciates
Supply of the euro increases; the euro depreciates
Demand for euro decreases; euro depreciates

assessment_items.question_content:
Contractionary fiscal policy in Maxistan has lowered the rate of inflation in Maxistan to $2\%$. The current rate of inflation in Ile is $10\%$.

**Which of the following graphs correctly shows the impact of the decrease in relative prices on the exchange rate for the Maxistan peso?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points of intersection. The vertical axis is labeled Ile dollar sign per peso, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript peso. One upward-sloping line is labeled S subscript peso. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled D subscript peso 1 and D subscript peso 2, with D subscript peso 2 to the right of D subscript peso 1. Lines S subscript peso and D subscript peso 1 intersect at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Lines S subscript peso and D subscript peso 2 intersect at the exchange rate E R subscript 2. Dashed lines extend horizontally from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the intersections. A right arrow points from D subscript peso 1 to D subscript peso 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9ea82d4a444ada8efa2c05044c337743351169a9)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points of intersection. The vertical axis is labeled peso per I l e dollar sign, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript I l e dollar sign. One upward-sloping line is labeled S subscript I l e dollar sign. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled D subscript I l e dollar sign 1 and D subscript I l e dollar sign 2, with D subscript I l e dollar sign 2 to the right of D subscript I l e dollar sign 1. Lines S subscript I l e dollar sign and D subscript I l e dollar sign 1 intersect at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Lines S subscript I l e dollar sign and D subscript I l e dollar sign 2 intersect at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Dashed lines extend horizontally from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the intersections. A right arrow points from D subscript I l e dollar sign 1 to D subscript I l e dollar sign 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/898938a4bf8d4dc8ccb208243f0ba8f36ae35c48)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines and two labeled points of intersection. The vertical axis is labeled E R dollar sign, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript money. One upward-sloping line is labeled S. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled D subscript 1 and D subscript 2, with D subscript 2 to the right of D subscript 1. Lines S and D subscript 1 intersect at exchange rate E R subscript 1. Lines S and D subscript 2 intersect at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Dashed lines extend horizontally from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the intersections. A right arrow points from D subscript 1 to D subscript 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/862200d175c5de1450c2eebf10d3e5e55c8c8877)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: D subscript I l e 2, S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1, and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript I l e dollar, and the vertical axis is labeled peso per I l e dollar. The axes are unmarked. Line D subscript I l e 2 is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 are upward-sloping from bottom left to top right, with S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 to the right of S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1. A rightward arrow is placed between the supply curves. S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 intersects D subscript I l e 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 1. S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 intersects D subscript I l e 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from both E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56ab25200a697759a82cc3f25796ef37d8b6d21e)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: D subscript I l e, S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1, and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript I l e dollar, and the vertical axis is labeled peso per I l e dollar. The axes are unmarked. Line D subscript I l e is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 are upward-sloping from bottom left to top right, with S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 to the left of S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1. A leftward arrow is placed between the supply curves. S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 intersects D subscript I l e at exchange rate E R subscript 1. S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 intersects D subscript I l e at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from both E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cf90f14f6126d8cd7b894e3220b8cc8328cfdf19)

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Atlantis engaged in expansionary fiscal policy that resulted in an increase in the price level.

**What will be the impact of the increase in the price level in Atlantis on the foreign exchange market for the Atlantian dollar and the exchange rate for the Atlantian dollar?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for the Atlantian dollar decreases; the Atlantian dollar depreciates
The demand for the Atlantian dollar increases; the Atlantian dollar appreciates
The supply of the Atlantian dollar decreases; the Atlantian dollar appreciates
The supply of the Atlantian dollar decreases; the Atlantian dollar depreciates
An increase in the price level in Atlantis will have no impact on the foreign exchange market for the Atlantian dollar

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Johnsrudia engaged in contractionary fiscal policy that resulted in a decrease in the price level.

**What will be the impact of the decrease in the relative price level in Johnsrudia on the foreign exchange market for the Johnrsrudian dollar and the exchange rate for the Johnrsrudian dollar?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand for the Johnrsrudian dollar increases; the Johnrsrudian dollar appreciates
The demand for the Johnrsrudian dollar decreases; the Johnrsrudian dollar depreciates
The supply of the Johnrsrudian dollar increases; the Johnrsrudian dollar depreciates
The supply of the Johnrsrudian dollar increases; the value of the Johnsrudian dollar doesn’t change
An increase in the price level in Johnsrudia will have no impact on the foreign exchange market for the Johnrsrudian dollar

assessment_items.question_content:
Ghana’s currency is the cedi, and assume Ghana has no currency controls. A change in the economy has led to the shift from point X to point Y. India is one of Ghana’s top trading partners, and India’s currency is the rupee. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cfe2ca176472e541e1f84e4066356de966bc8aa9)

**What's the impact of this economic change on the values of the cedi and rupee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: cedi depreciates; rupee appreciates
cedi appreciates; rupee appreciates
cedi appreciates; rupee depreciates
cedi depreciates; rupee depreciates
cedi is unaffected; rupee appreciates

assessment_items.question_content:
The citizens of Burginland are convinced that there will never be an improvement in the economic situation there and that there are likely to be widespread defaults on bonds and bank failures. As a result, they want to buy more financial assets from their trading partner, Marthaland. The currency in Burginland is the eagle and the currency in Marthaland is the jean.

**What is the effect of the increased demand for Marthaland’s financial assets on the real value of each country’s currency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Burginland eagle depreciates and the Marthland jean appreciates.
The Burginland eagle appreciates and the Marthland jean depreciates.
The Burginland eagle appreciates and the Marthland jean appreciates.
The Burginland eagle is not affected and the Marthland jean appreciates.
The Burginland eagle appreciates and the Marthland jean is not affected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What effect does this have in Marthaland on real interest rates and the value of Marthaland’s currency in the short run and economic growth in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real interest rates decrease, Marthaland’s currency depreciates, and economic growth increases.
Real interest rates decrease, Marthaland’s currency appreciates, and economic growth decreases.
Real interest rates increase, Marthaland’s currency depreciates, and economic growth increases.
Real interest rates increase, Marthaland’s currency depreciates, and economic growth isn’t affected.
Real interest rates decrease, Marthaland’s currency isn’t affected, and economic growth isn’t affected.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Assuming there is no change in the nominal exchange rate, what will be the effect of contractionary monetary policy in Canada on the Canadian price level and the real exchange rate (RER) of the Canadian dollar?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To find the real exchange rate for the Canadian dollar, you would use this formula:

$RER_{CAN\$} = \text{ amount of other currency per Canadian dollar} \times \dfrac{PL_{CAN}}{PL_{\text{other country}}}$

choices.content: Price level will decrease; RER will decrease.
Price level will increase; RER will decrease.
Price level will decrease; RER will remain unchanged.
Price level will increase; RER will increase.
Price level will decrease; RER will increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
Expansionary monetary policy in Maxistan has increased the rate of inflation in Maxistan to $12\%$. The current rate of inflation in Ile is $1\%$.

**Which of the following graphs correctly shows the impact of the decrease in relative prices on the exchange rate for the Maxistan peso?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: S subscript peso, D subscript peso subscript 1, and D subscript peso subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript peso, and the vertical axis is labeled I l e dollar per peso. The axes are unmarked. Line S subscript peso is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Lines D subscript peso subscript 1 and D subscript peso subscript 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with D subscript peso subscript 2 to the left of D subscript peso subscript 1. A leftward arrow is placed between the demand curves. S subscript peso intersects D subscript peso subscript 1 at exchange rate E R subscript 1 and intersects D subscript peso subscript 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from both E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/946e7b20efc036a91f65471c64622d52821a28c1)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: S subscript I l e dollar, D subscript I l e dollar subscript 1, and D subscript I l e dollar subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript I l e dollar, and the vertical axis is labeled peso per I l e dollar. The axes are unmarked. Line S subscript I l e dollar is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Lines D subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 and D subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with D subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 to the right of D subscript I l e dollar subscript 1. A rightward arrow is placed between the demand curves. S subscript I l e dollar intersects D subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 at exchange rate E R subscript 1 and intersects D subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from both E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e8ad8981632033839bde61a93c8e89f4b1c8e3cc)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: S, D subscript 1, and D subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript money, and the vertical axis is labeled E R dollar. The axes are unmarked. Line S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Lines D subscript 1 and D subscript 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with D subscript 2 to the right of D subscript 1. A rightward arrow is placed between the demand curves. S intersects D subscript 1 at exchange rate E R subscript 1 and intersects D subscript 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from both E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/862200d175c5de1450c2eebf10d3e5e55c8c8877)

![A coordinate plane plots three labeled straight lines: D subscript I l e 2, S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1, and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript I l e dollar, and the vertical axis is labeled peso per I l e dollar. The axes are unmarked. Line D subscript I l e 2 is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 are upward-sloping from bottom left to top right, with S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 to the right of S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1. A rightward arrow is placed between the supply curves. S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 intersects D subscript I l e 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 1. S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 intersects D subscript I l e 2 at exchange rate E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from both E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56ab25200a697759a82cc3f25796ef37d8b6d21e)

![A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines and two points on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled peso per I l e dollar, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q subscript I l e dollar. One downward-sloping line is labeled D subscript Ile. Two upward-sloping lines are labeled S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2, with S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 to the left of S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1. The intersection of D subscript I l e and S subscript I l e dollar subscript 1 is marked as E R subscript 1, and the intersection of D subscript I l e with S subscript I l e dollar subscript 2 is marked as E R subscript 2. Horizontal dashed lines extend from E R subscript 1 and E R subscript 2 to the vertical axis. A leftward-pointing arrow connects the two supply curves.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9046aa788d0a2d3c516e540e4be6174c00ad440b)

assessment_items.question_content:
An increase in business optimism in Maxistan has changed the real interest rate in Maxistan which has resulted in a change in the flow of financial assets between Maxistan and its trading partners and impacted the value of Maxistan’s currency.

**Which of the following describes an appropriate action that the central bank of Maxistan could take to offset the change in the value of Maxistan’s currency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sell its currency or buy another country’s currency.
Buy its currency or sell another country’s currency.
Sell bonds
Increase the demand for money
Increase interest payments made on excess reserves held by banks
